_is_html_file#
_FlatDirectorySourcez
Link source specified by ``--find-links=<path-to-dir>``.
    This looks the content of the directory, and returns:
    * ``page_candidates``: Links listed on each HTML file in the directory.
    * ``file_candidates``: Archives in the directory.
    N)
candidates_from_page
pathr
_candidates_from_page
pathlib

_FlatDirectorySource.__init__r
_FlatDirectorySource.linkc
iterdirr
strr 
urlr
z$_FlatDirectorySource.page_candidatesc
_FlatDirectorySource.file_links)
__doc__
CandidatesFromPager-
_LocalFileSourceaC
``--find-links=<path-or-url>`` or ``--[extra-]index-url=<path-or-url>``.
    If a URL is supplied, it must be a ``file:`` URL. If a path is supplied to
    the option, it is converted to a URL first. This returns:
    * ``page_candidates``: Links listed on an HTML file.
    * ``file_candidates``: The non-HTML file.
    N
_link
_LocalFileSource.__init__r
_LocalFileSource.linkc
z _LocalFileSource.page_candidatesc
_LocalFileSource.file_links
_RemoteFileSourcez
``--find-links=<url>`` or ``--[extra-]index-url=<url>``.
    This returns:
    * ``page_candidates``: Links listed on an HTML file.
    * ``file_candidates``: The non-HTML file.
    N)
page_validatorr
_page_validatorr5
_RemoteFileSource.__init__r
_RemoteFileSource.linkc
z!_RemoteFileSource.page_candidatesc
_RemoteFileSource.file_links)
PageValidatorr
_IndexDirectorySourcez
``--[extra-]index-url=<path-to-directory>``.
    This is treated like a remote URL; ``candidates_from_page`` contains logic
    for this by appending ``index.html`` to the link.
    Nr3
_IndexDirectorySource.__init__r
_IndexDirectorySource.linkc
z%_IndexDirectorySource.page_candidatesc
